title: Inbox Triage and Reply Drafts for Agencies
metaDescription: Your inbox triaged, drafts written for routine replies in your voice, and high-priority items surfaced. We install the workflow so the inbox stops eating mornings.
excerpt: The inbox eats the morning at most agencies because each message gets reprocessed from scratch. We install a triage layer that surfaces the few that need you and drafts the rest.

sections.intro:
The inbox eats the morning at most agencies because each message gets reprocessed from scratch. The owner reads, decides, drafts, sometimes punts, sometimes forgets. The high-stakes messages get the same processing as the renewals questions and the calendar reschedules.

We install a triage layer in front of the inbox that does two things: surfaces the few messages that actually need your judgment, and drafts replies in your voice for the routine ones so you only review and send.

This is a Capabilities layer of an AI Operating System.
sections.fourCFit:
Inbox triage sits in Capabilities. It depends on:

  • Connections: your inbox, CRM, calendar, project tool.
  • Context: your voice, your usual decisions on each common message type, your priorities.
  • Cadence: optional morning digest that consolidates everything you need to act on, in one place.

See it in context at the AI Operating System for Marketing Agencies pillar.
sections.whatDoneLooksLike:
• Every new inbound email is classified: high-priority (needs you), drafted (system has a reply ready), or auto-handled (calendar shuffles, status updates).
  • Drafts appear in your inbox under each thread, in your voice, ready to review or edit.
  • High-priority messages are surfaced with one-line context ("Client X is asking about a delayed deliverable; here is the relevant history").
  • Optional 7am digest that consolidates the day's priorities and pending drafts in one place.
sections.howWeInstall:
1. Audit, free, about 10 minutes. Run the audit.
  2. Install, inside 10 business days. We connect your inbox, train the classifier on the message types you actually see, and capture your voice for drafts.
  3. Operate, ongoing. See plans.
sections.expectedResults:
• Hours per day on the inbox: from 2 to 3 hours down to 30 to 60 minutes.
  • High-priority messages caught: from "sometimes I miss them under the noise" to "surfaced first, every time."
  • Reply time on routine messages: minutes, not hours.

Standing guarantee on the wider AIOS engagement: recover 40 or more hours a month, or we keep working free until you do.
sections.faq:
What if I do not trust drafts written for me?

You should not trust them on day 1. Reviewing every draft is the right starting posture. After a week or two of edits, the voice gets accurate enough that most drafts go out unedited.

What about sensitive emails?

Sensitive threads can be excluded by sender, by label, or by keyword. The system never drafts on threads you mark off-limits.

Who owns the system?

You do. Every prompt and connection lives in your accounts.
